alkylation reagent: alkylated with reduction solution 2 (50‐mM iodoacetamide and 25‐mM NH4HCO3)
alkylation concentration: alkylated with reduction solution 2 (50‐mM iodoacetamide and 25‐mM NH4HCO3)
cleavage agent: Trypsin was chosen as the enzyme with a maximum of two missed cleavages.
collision energy: peptides were fragmented with HCD and normalized collision energy (NCE) 28.
instrument: analyzed by mass spectroscopy using a Q‐Exactive mass spectrometer.
labeling: biotinylated protein can be detected after a short period of biotin treatment.
reduction reagent: 1‐mM DTT, 1‐mM PMSF, leupeptin, protease inhibitor cocktail.
reduction concentration: 1‐mM DTT, 1‐mM PMSF, leupeptin, protease inhibitor cocktail.
